home_h1_1: Rehabilitation and Empowerment of IDF Wounded and Terror Victims
home_h2_2: Our Mission: Restoring Hope Through Sport
home_h2_7: Success Stories - Voices of Our Participants
home_h2_8: Join Us on the Rehabilitation Journey
home_h3_9: 19 Years of Excellence in Sport-Based Rehabilitation - Restoring Hope and Strength to Our Hero Soldiers
home_p_14: Tikvot is a non-profit organization dedicated to rehabilitating IDF wounded and terror victims through bereaved families using therapeutic sport. For 19 years, we believe that sport is not just physical activity - it's a powerful tool for healing the soul, building self-confidence, and reconnecting to life.
home_p_15:
Since 2007, we believe that hope for a better life is not a dream, but a realistic and achievable goal. Through personalized sport programs that empower IDF wounded, terror victims and bereaved families through sport, we provide participants with a new path to life - a path of resilience, achievement and belonging.
The organization operates as a non-profit.
home_p_16:
In the transition from injury to athletic challenge
a new path is born. Through professional training and guidance, participants embark on a gradual journey where
the body and mind learn to work together again. This is a process that doesn't focus on forcefully breaking boundaries, but
on renewed acquaintance with capability, at a personal pace, and building trust along the way.



The program develops endurance, discipline
and mental strength, but above all it empowers through perseverance, faith and collaborative work. Progress
is measured not only in athletic achievements, but in the ability to sustain effort, cope with difficulty
and feel belonging - a reminder that true strength is built slowly, from within
home_p_17:
For many Tikvot participants,
entering the gym is a significant step far beyond physical training. This is a space where gradual return
to body control, sense of capability and building a new identity within a changed reality becomes possible.
Physical work is not disconnected from the mind, but serves as a daily anchor of stability, perseverance and self-connection.



Through personalized training
programs, we accompany participants in a process aimed at strengthening both physical and mental well-being.
Every movement, repetition and small progress contributes to building renewed self-confidence and the feeling
that the body can be a source of strength - not just injury
home_p_18: Equine-assisted therapy helps post-trauma victims through nervous system regulation, gentle body connection and creating a sense of security that is non-verbal. The rhythmic movement of the horse calms tension and anxiety, enables "here and now" presence and restores a sense of control. The relationship with the horse is non-judgmental, based on response to emotion rather than words, thus creating a safe space for trust, strengthening sense of capability and a corrective experience of influence, leadership and existence.
home_p_19: A dedicated program for reserve fighters dealing with physical and mental challenges after service. The program provides coping tools, community support and adapted sports activities.
home_p_20: A special program developed specifically for victims of October 7th events. We provide emotional support, rehabilitation activities and a supportive community for those who experienced the difficult experiences.
home_p_21: A comprehensive program for terror attack victims including physical and mental rehabilitation, complementary treatments and family support. We accompany participants at every stage of the recovery process.
home_p_22: "After my injury in the IDF, I thought my life was over. Tikvot showed me there is real hope. The equine therapy and the ski Olympics delegation completely changed my life. Today I feel strong, capable and full of purpose."
home_p_23: "As an attack victim, I dealt with severe trauma that wouldn't let me live. The Tikvot team, the amazing volunteers and the supportive community helped me find myself again. Sport became my way to cope and heal."
home_p_24: "Participating in the triathlon was an experience I never thought I could go through. Tikvot gave me the tools, training and support to reach the finish line. It's not just physical rehabilitation - it's rehabilitation of the soul and spirit."
home_p_25: Whether you are IDF wounded, terror victims, family members or potential volunteers - we have a place for you
home_alt_59: IDF wounded veterans participating in rehabilitative sports activities

home_span_28: Our Mission: Restoring Hope Through Sport
home_span_29: Our Mission: Restoring Hope Through Sport
home_span_30: Our Mission: Restoring Hope Through Sport
programs_h1_1: Our Rehabilitation Programs for IDF Wounded and Terror Victims
programs_p_15: For 18 years, the ski expeditions of Tikvot Association for IDF wounded and terror victims have been a space of confrontation, not escape. The encounter with the snowy mountain invites the body and soul to be present, to feel, and to move again within a challenging yet safe reality. Skiing is not a vacation - it is a process of reconnecting with the body, with capability, and with the moment when it becomes possible again to trust yourself.
programs_p_16:
In the transition from injury to athletic challenge, a new path is born. Through training and professional guidance, participants embark on a gradual journey where body and soul learn to work together again. This is a process that is not about forcefully breaking boundaries, but about renewed acquaintance with capability, at a personal pace, and building trust along the way.

The program develops endurance, discipline and mental strength, but above all it empowers through perseverance, faith and collaborative work. Progress is not measured only in athletic achievements, but in the ability to sustain effort, cope with difficulty and feel belonging - a reminder that true strength is built slowly, from within
programs_p_17:
For many Tikvot participants, entering the gym is a significant step far beyond physical training. This is a space where gradual return to body control, sense of capability and building a new identity within a changed reality becomes possible. Physical work is not disconnected from the soul, but serves as a daily anchor of stability, perseverance and self-connection.

Through personalized training, we accompany participants in a process aimed at strengthening both physical and mental well-being. Every movement, repetition and small progress contributes to building renewed self-confidence and the feeling that the body can be a source of strength - not just injury
programs_p_18:
Horse-assisted therapy helps post-trauma victims through nervous system regulation, gentle body connection and creating a sense of security that is non-verbal. The rhythmic movement of the horse calms tension and anxiety, enables "here and now" presence and restores a sense of control.
The connection with the horse is a non-judgmental relationship, based on response to emotion rather than words, thus creating a safe space for trust, strengthening sense of capability and a corrective experience of influence, leadership and existence.
programs_p_19: The go-karting experience offers a precise encounter with movement, concentration and control, adapted to the physical and emotional needs of participants. For IDF wounded and terror victims, these are not just hours of respite from rehabilitation routine, but a space where body and soul meet again within a safe and defined challenge. The track demands full presence, real-time decision making and attention to self and environment.
programs_p_20: Beyond general programs, we offer dedicated programs tailored to the specific needs of different groups:
programs_p_21: A special program for reserve soldiers dealing with physical and mental challenges after long service periods. The program includes group sports activities, stress management and coping workshops, and community support.
programs_p_22: We understand the unique challenges of reserve soldiers - the transition between civilian life and service, the difficulty of reintegrating after extended service periods, and the need for ongoing support. Our program provides a comprehensive response to all these needs.
programs_p_23: A special rehabilitation program developed specifically for victims of October 7th events. The program provides emotional support, personalized rehabilitation activities, complementary treatments and a supportive community of people who have experienced similar experiences.
programs_p_24: We understand that the experiences endured by victims of these difficult events require a sensitive, professional and personally tailored approach. Our staff has undergone special training and works in collaboration with professional entities to provide the best possible response.
programs_p_25: A comprehensive program for terror attack victims including physical and mental rehabilitation, complementary treatments such as horse therapy, adapted sports activities and family support. We accompany participants at every stage of the recovery process and provide long-term support.
programs_p_26: Terror victims face unique challenges - physical and mental trauma, difficulties in daily functioning, fears and anxieties. Our program focuses on building resilience, restoring sense of control and security, and creating a supportive community.
programs_p_27: We understand that family is an integral part of the rehabilitation process. Therefore, we offer support programs for family members of our participants as well. Workshops, support groups and joint family activities help families cope with challenges and support their loved ones in the best way possible.
programs_p_28: If you are IDF wounded, terror victims or family members - we are here for you. Contact us to receive additional information about programs, registration process and beginning the journey toward rehabilitation and empowerment.
programs_h3_13: A variety of personalized rehabilitation solutions combining sports, therapy and community support
contact_us_h1_1: Contact Tikvot - We'd Love to Hear from You
contact_us_h3_7: Whether you're interested in joining as participants, volunteers, or supporters - we're here for you
contact_us_p_20: Fill out the form and we'll get back to you as soon as possible
contact_us_p_21: We're available through various channels - choose the most convenient way for you:
contact_us_p_22: IDF wounded veterans and terror victims are invited to contact us through the form, by phone, or via WhatsApp. We'll conduct an introductory conversation, understand your needs, and find the most suitable program. The process includes initial assessment, building a personal plan, and starting activities.
contact_us_p_23: No! All our programs are provided free of charge to participants. Tikvot is a non-profit organization that operates on donations and volunteering. We believe every IDF wounded veteran and terror victim deserves support regardless of financial ability.
contact_us_p_24: We'd be happy to have you! Contact us and we'll explain the various volunteering opportunities - sports instructors, personal companions, administrative support, and more. We'll hold an introductory meeting, understand your skills and availability, and find the right role.
contact_us_p_25: Absolutely! We believe family is an integral part of the rehabilitation process. Many of our activities include family members, and we also offer dedicated support programs for families.
contact_us_p_26: There are many ways to support - financial donations, sports equipment donations, organizing fundraising events, business partnerships, and more. Every donation, large or small, helps us continue operating and restoring hope to IDF wounded veterans.
contact_us_p_27: Our activities take place in various locations throughout the country at diverse times. Each program is tailored to participants' needs. Contact us for detailed information about upcoming activities.
contact_us_p_28: IDF wounded veterans and terror victims are invited to join our rehabilitation programs. We'll find the right program for you together and accompany you every step of the way.
contact_us_p_29: Join our volunteer team and contribute your time, skills, and heart. Volunteering with Tikvot is a meaningful and enriching experience.
contact_us_p_30: Your donation enables us to continue operating and restoring hope to IDF wounded veterans. Every donation makes a real difference.

programs_page_title: Comprehensive Rehabilitation Programs for IDF Wounded — TikvotTikvot
home_meta_description: Tikvot offers rehabilitation for IDF wounded soldiers and terror victims through sports. Horse therapy, ski expeditions and triathlon, reserve soldier programs.
about_the_founder_h1_1: Simona: The Beating Heart Behind IDF Wounded Rehabilitation
about_the_founder_h2_2: A Story of Mission and Giving
about_the_founder_h2_4: "The injury is not the end of the story, but the beginning of a new chapter"
about_the_founder_h2_5: Looking Forward: The Vision for the Future
about_the_founder_p_11: For over two decades, Simona has dedicated her life to one single purpose: restoring hope to the lives of those who sacrificed their bodies and souls for the security of the state. Her vision has become a life-changing reality for hundreds of fighters.
about_the_founder_p_12: The story of Tikvot Association is inseparably woven with Simona's life story. Growing up in a home where Zionist values and love of the land were guiding principles, the connection to the IDF and security forces was natural for her from a young age. However, it was one defining event, a chance encounter in a rehabilitation hospital with a group of young IDF wounded, that changed the course of her life forever.
about_the_founder_p_13: The story of Tikvot Association is inseparably woven with Simona's life story. Growing up in a home where Zionist values and love of the land were guiding principles, the connection to the IDF and security forces was natural for her from a young age. However, it was one defining event, a chance encounter in a rehabilitation hospital with a group of young IDF wounded, that changed the course of her life forever.
about_the_founder_p_14: In that encounter, Simona identified a huge and painful gap. While the medical system provided excellent physical treatment, the fighters' souls often remained bleeding. She understood that true rehabilitation of IDF wounded does not end with bone healing or physical scar repair, but requires rebuilding the sense of capability, self-confidence, and hope for the future. This understanding was the spark that led to the organization's establishment.
about_the_founder_p_15: From then until today, Simona leads the association with a firm hand but endless sensitivity. She believes that challenging sports is a first-class rehabilitation tool. Through activities like skiing, mountain climbing, and cycling, she enables terror victims and rehabilitated fighters to rediscover the forces within them. For her, every smile of a fighter who has returned to stand on their feet (literally and figuratively) is a whole world.
about_the_founder_p_16: The belief that there is no limit to human capability. Simona pushes IDF wounded to face complex physical challenges, not just as sport, but as a way to rehabilitate the self-confidence damaged following the injury.
about_the_founder_p_17: For Simona, the association is not an institution but a home. Every fighter and every terror victim who joins the activities becomes part of a supportive, embracing, and understanding family that accompanies them throughout the entire journey.
about_the_founder_p_18: Love of the land and gratitude to those who protect it are the foundation of all activities. The deep connection to IDF values and Israeli combat heritage is present in every activity and every meeting of the association.
about_the_founder_p_19: This sentence, common in Simona's speech, constitutes the central motto of her activities. She sees each IDF wounded person as a hero who needs the right tools to return and triumph - this time in the arena of civilian life.
about_the_founder_p_20: The unique approach she developed combines uncompromising demands for excellence with a warm and loving embrace. She knows when to push the fighters to the edge of their capability so they can prove to themselves they are capable, and when to provide quiet and containing support in difficult moments.
about_the_founder_p_21: Beyond physical rehabilitation, Simona places great emphasis on reintegrating terror victims and wounded back into society, studies, and the workforce. She works tirelessly with governmental and private entities to open doors for those who paid such a dear price.
about_the_founder_p_22: Even after years of blessed work, Simona doesn't rest for a moment. The complex Israeli reality continues to create new challenges, and the family of IDF wounded unfortunately continues to expand. Her vision for the coming years includes expanding the activity center, establishing additional support groups for wounded families, and deepening cooperation with various IDF units.
about_the_founder_p_23: She works to raise public awareness of the importance of mental rehabilitation alongside physical rehabilitation, and leads campaigns for recognition of the rights of terror victims and fighters. For Simona, as long as there is a soldier who needs help to smile again, the mission is not yet complete.
about_the_founder_p_24: Simona's and Tikvot Association's work is made possible thanks to good people who understand the magnitude of the hour. We invite you to take part in this wonderful journey and be partners in the path.

programs_meta_description: Comprehensive rehabilitation programs for IDF wounded and terror victims: equine therapy, international ski expeditions, triathlon, races and special programs.
about_the_association_h1_1: About Tikvot Association - 19 Years of Rehabilitating IDF Wounded
about_the_association_p_24:
Tikvot Association is a non-profit organization
that works to rehabilitate IDF wounded, terror attack victims, and bereaved families through sports
and action. Our goal is to transform trauma
into victory, and to restore hope and confidence to those who were injured.

The association, founded in 2007, has treated
more than 14,000 wounded to date. It accompanies IDF wounded and terror survivors from the moment of injury,
throughout the rehabilitation process and until recovery.

The therapeutic tools we provide: water skiing, horseback
riding, go-karting, training and participation in
triathlon competitions, cycling, surfing and more.

Twice
a year the association goes out for skiing and snowboarding activities at ski resorts around the world with certified instructors
and medical staff.

The delegation
is built from wounded who are medically approved to go out for such challenging sports activities. This activity proves
to them again - there is no peak that cannot be conquered.

The events of October 7th left a deep
and lasting mark on Israeli society. Many soldiers and civilians fought for Israel and now we
are fighting for their rehabilitation.&  trauma
into victory, and to restore hope and confidence to those who were injured.

The association, founded in 2007, has treated
more than 14,000 wounded to date. It accompanies IDF wounded and terror survivors from the moment of injury,
throughout the rehabilitation process and until recovery.

The therapeutic tools we provide: water skiing, horseback
riding, go-karting, training and participation in
triathlon competitions, cycling, surfing and more.
Twice
a year the association goes out for skiing and snowboarding activities at ski resorts around the world with certified instructors
and medical staff.
The delegation
is built from wounded who are medically approved to go out for such challenging sports activities. This activity proves
to them again - there is no peak that cannot be conquered.

The events of October 7th left a deep
and lasting mark on Israeli society. Many soldiers and civilians fought for Israel and now we
are fighting for their rehabilitation.
about_the_association_p_25: We are committed to providing the best service to IDF wounded and terror victims. Every decision we make is guided by one question: what is best for our participants?
about_the_association_p_26: We believe in the power of community. Our participants don't just receive services - they become part of a large and supportive family that stays with them over time.
about_the_association_p_27: We strive for excellence in everything we do. Our team and volunteers receive professional training and work according to the highest standards.
about_the_association_p_28: Tikvot operates on a completely voluntary basis. We believe that giving from the heart brings the best results and strengthens the connection between volunteers and participants.
about_the_association_p_29: We believe that every person is unique and requires a personally adapted approach. We invest in developing innovative programs and adapt them to the specific needs of each participant.
about_the_association_p_30: Our name, Tikvot, reflects the core of what we do - we restore hope to people who have experienced difficult experiences and empower them to believe in their abilities.
about_the_association_p_31: Tikvot operates thanks to hundreds of dedicated volunteers who invest their time, skills and hearts. Our team includes:
about_the_association_p_32: Our management team is composed of volunteers with rich experience in managing non-profit organizations, working with special populations and developing rehabilitation programs. They lead Tikvot's vision and ensure that we continue to provide service at the highest level.
about_the_association_p_33: Our instructors are certified and experienced professionals in various sports fields - skiing, triathlon, swimming, riding and more. They have all undergone special training in working with special populations and understand the unique challenges of IDF wounded and terror victims.
about_the_association_p_34: Our therapeutic team includes psychologists, equine therapists, physiotherapists and other professionals. They all volunteer their time and provide professional services at the highest level.
about_the_association_p_35: Hundreds of additional volunteers accompany our participants at events, assist in organizing activities, provide emotional support and create the warm and supportive community that is the heart of Tikvot.
about_the_association_p_36: We are always looking for additional volunteers who want to be part of our mission. Whether you have specific professional skills or simply a desire to help and contribute - we have a place for you. Volunteering at Tikvot is an enriching and meaningful experience that changes lives - both of the participants and of the volunteers themselves.
about_the_association_p_37: Throughout our 20 years of activity, we have achieved a lot. But our greatest achievement is not measurable in numbers - it's the change we see in the lives of our participants.
about_the_association_p_38: Tikvot has received recognition and appreciation from many bodies, including the IDF, Ministry of Defense, international Paralympic sports organizations and more. But the most meaningful recognition for us comes from the participants and their families - their stories, their achievements and the change they undergo.
about_the_association_p_39: Tikvot operates in cooperation with many organizations and bodies that support our mission and help us provide the best service to participants:
about_the_association_p_40: We thank all our partners and supporters for their trust and support. Without you, we could not continue to operate and restore hope to our heroic soldiers.
about_the_association_p_41: We look forward with hope and determination. Our goal is to continue and expand our activities, reach more IDF wounded and terror victims, and develop new and advanced programs.
about_the_association_p_42: We believe that every IDF wounded and terror victim deserves to receive the support and tools to restore life to full and meaningful function. Tikvot will continue to operate tirelessly to turn this vision into reality.
about_the_association_p_43: Whether you are interested in joining as participants, volunteers or supporters - we would be happy to hear from you
about_the_association_h3_16: Our story, our values, and our commitment to our heroic soldiers

about_the_association_li_82: International collaborations and transferring our knowledge and experience to other organizations

Twice
a year the association goes on skiing and snowboarding activities at ski resorts around the world with certified instructors
and medical staff.

about_the_association_span_53:
The delegation
consists of wounded who are medically approved to participate in such challenging sports activities. This activity proves
to them again - there is no peak that cannot be conquered.

about_the_association_span_55:
The events of October 7th left a deep
and lasting impact on Israeli society. Many soldiers and civilians fought for Israel and now we
are fighting for their rehabilitation
about_the_association_span_56:
The association, founded in 2007, has treated
more than 14,000 wounded to date. It accompanies IDF wounded and terror survivors from the moment of injury,
throughout the rehabilitation process and until recovery
about_the_association_span_57:
Therapeutic tools we provide: water skiing, horseback
riding, go-karting, training and participation in
triathlon competitions, cycling, surfing and more.

about_the_founder_meta_description: Meet Simona, the founder and driving force behind Tikvot Association. A story of vision, giving, and unwavering commitment to rehabilitating IDF wounded veterans and terror victims.
about_the_association_meta_description: Tikvot - 20 years of excellence in rehabilitating IDF wounded and terror victims through sports. A volunteer-based organization restoring hope to our heroic soldiers.
